label: noncommutation
headline_statement: Refuted: the strict-existence clause is false under the shared pointwise-CDF band definition. In the two-action, three-outcome, one-covariate-cell MSM response-type LP with strict overlap and any \(1<\Gamma<\infty\), the monotone upper-tail MSM tilt is a common first-order-stochastically maximal vertex for every threshold and every generated loss \((Y-\eta)_+\). Hence \(W_{\tau,\Gamma}^{\mathrm{band}}(\pi;P)=\underline W_{\tau,\Gamma}(\pi;P)\) and \(\Delta_{\mathrm{nc}}(\pi;P)=0\) throughout that proposed witness class, so no member of that class can witness \(\Delta_{\mathrm{nc}}(\pi;P)>0\).
witness_construction_if_refuted: Generic finite-LP certificate for the proposed witness class: with X degenerate, A={0,1}, Y in {0,1,2}, strict overlap e_a in (0,1), and 1<Gamma<infty, the MSM cell LP is q_aj=p_aj r_aj, ell_a<=r_aj<=u_a, sum_j p_aj r_aj=1. Greedy allocation from the lower face to the largest outcomes gives a single upper-tail vertex q_a^star that minimizes every lower CDF threshold and maximizes every generated loss (Y-eta)_+. The policy mixture of these vertices is a CDF in the pointwise band and is first-order-stochastically maximal, so the band inner supremum equals the nested tilted-loss endpoint for every eta. Therefore W_band=underline W and Delta_nc=0 for every law in the stipulated two-action, three-outcome, one-cell class. For e_0=e_1=1/2, Gamma=2, pi=(1/2,1/2), and uniform observed cell probabilities, q_a^star=(1/4,1/4,1/2) and both endpoints equal 2 at tau=1/2.
